title: The four tone marks
pedagogy: Tone rules (3/4): tone mark names and shapes — mai ek (◌่), mai tho (◌้), mai tri (◌๊), mai chattawa (◌๋). On MID-class consonants they give low, falling, high, rising respectively.

exercises.title: Marks that steer the tone
exercises.thaiExample: ก่า ก้า ก๊า ก๋า
body: Four small marks sit above the initial consonant and override the default tone.
On a MID-class consonant they are perfectly regular, in naming order: mai ek ◌่ → low, mai tho ◌้ → falling, mai tri ◌๊ → high, mai chattawa ◌๋ → rising.
On high- and low-class consonants the same marks land differently — that's the next lesson. First, learn their names and shapes cold.

exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Mid class + mai ek = low tone.

exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Mid class + mai tho = falling tone.

exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Mid class + mai tri = high tone.

exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Mid class + mai chattawa = rising tone.
